entry.title: Custom fields are not visible in screen maintain business partner.
entry.content:
Unable to Add Custom fields in the Maintain Business Partner App


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom fields are not visible in screen maintain busi...
entry.content:
The Maintain Business Partner app cannot support custom fields, For maintaining the custom fields use the Business Partner Fiori apps (Manage Business Partner Master Data, Manage Customer Master Data and Manage Supplier Master Data).

Refer this KBA for more details : Unable to Add Custom fields in the Maintain Business Partner App

entry.title: Cant Download Base64 Files in SAP Build Apps on Mobile? It only take 1 min to do, Vote to Fix It!!
entry.content:
I’ve raised a feature request on the SAP Influence portal to enable Base64 file downloads in native mobile apps (Android/iOS) using SAP Build Apps. As of now, this functionality is only supported on web apps, which limits real-world mobile use cases like:

✅ Downloading PDFs, receipts, reports, CSVs on the go
✅ Offline access for field workers
✅ Handling file attachments directly from CAP/HANA Cloud responses
✅ Avoiding complex native plugin workarounds in a no-code/low-code platform

This improvement would allow smoother file handling from SAP Build Apps → CAP → mobile, making the ecosystem much more developer- and business-friendly.

📌 Please take a moment to upvote the request here:
👉 https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/346243

Let’s show SAP how much the community needs this!

💬 Feel free to drop your thoughts, use cases, or support in the comments below!

#SAPBuildApps #SAPBTP #SAPCAP #HANACloud #LowCode #MobileApps #SAPInfluence #FioriMobile #Base64Download #SAPCommunity SAP Build Apps

entry.title: Re: Production order GR .Document XXXXXXX does not contai...
entry.content: This SAP note talks about purchase orders, while the issue relates to production orders

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS "AI in Sourcing and Procurement" (Virtual, May 6, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

May 06, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on AI in Sourcing and Procurement taking place virtually on May 6th, 2025. Please find the current agenda here.

The workshop will start with the session that explores the AI capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Sourcing and Procurement, powered by SAP HANA for Machine Learning (ML) and SAP Business Artificial Intelligence (AI). Discover how embedded ML algorithms in SAP HANA can enhance existing business processes, driving greater efficiency and fostering innovation at scale.

Next, the workshop will provide a comprehensive overview of the Innovative Solution Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework, designed to support SAP S/4HANA embedded scenarios such as Contract Consumption and Prediction of Delivery Date. Participants will explore the strategic role of AI in strengthening the digital core, unlocking opportunities for businesses to stay competitive and meet evolving market demands.

Additionally, the workshop will delve into the Joule AI Copilot, featuring detailed use-case examples for Operational Purchasers, including Purchase Requisition Creation and Purchase Order Change.

Finally, a dedicated session will showcase AI use cases in SAP Ariba, highlighting its latest Joule capabilities.

At the end, the workshop will present the comprehensive SAP MaxAttention service portfolio for AI, designed to support customers in exploring, innovating, and effectively implementing AI capabilities. The session will also highlight how customers can leverage the SAP MaxAttention methodology to develop their Custom AI solutions in collaboration with the SAP Custom Innovation Services organization.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi again @poojarychetan ,

I'm afraid your questions goes out of scope of this blog post. Please take up compliance, governance and authorization questions with your security team and/or a SAP Security architect.

The simple quick answer I can give is that business users not usually get access to application job apps. But discuss your exact requirement/use-case, as mentioned.

Teams involved again depends on the requirement. It could be Finance or Procurement needing background handling and potentially wanting to see logs/statistics. The parts that I just covered are the developer. When it comes to especially recurring scheduling, that should be checked by whoever is in charge of your systems operations, which then takes us back to the first question, who should be able to do it, and as said, that is not my scope here, thanks 😊

entry.title: Re: An error occurred during the import of fixed asset po...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP community blog.

 

You're getting this error because the transaction you're trying to post happens before the legacy asset transfer date you've set in the system - which is December 31, 2024 for asset 1510 20000025-0.

In SAP, once you've done a legacy transfer, the system doesn't allow you to post anything before that transfer date unless you're doing a mid-year transfer, and even then, it still has to be within the same fiscal year.

Since your transfer date is right at the end of the year (12/31/2024), SAP expects that all new transactions start from January 1, 2025 onward. That’s why your posting is being rejected.

 

What you can do:

Option 1: Adjust your posting date

Just change the posting date of your transaction to any date from 2025 onward. That should fix the issue if you're just trying to record normal activity after the legacy upload.

 

Option 2: If you do need to post something in 2024

Then you'll need to redo your asset legacy transfer as a mid-year transfer (like 30.06.2024 or earlier), and make sure your values reflect that earlier date. This allows you to post things in the second half of 2024.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
No Problem @mlauber : This helps 🙂

Thankyou again .

entry.title: Re: Cloud API Enablement - FAQ
entry.content:
Hello @AlexanderRother @OlgaDolinskaja 

We have a requirement where we need to create multiple process orders and as the BAPI_PROCORD_CREATE is not release ( has a CALL-SCREEN in it 😕 ) we call it async in application jobs. 

To display the results, we created few custom entities and read the job logs. 

We're now wondering if the custom entities are considered Tier 1 or Tier 2? Is this approach in line with Clean Core guidelines? 

Thank you,
Lucian

entry.title: Integration Suite - iSeries (AS400) DB2 JDBC connection
entry.content:
Hi

Is it possible to install a JDBC driver to connect to the IBM iSeries DB2 database?

I have not been able to find any such driver which will deploy without the "Message: (deployJdbcDriver)Failed to deploy jdbc driver null for tenant" error message.

The links to the IBM website for drivers db2Driver11.5.4 / db2Driver11.5.5 in Note 3314847 are impossible to navigate.
In addition, there is a rodmap item for Q4 this year, for "JDBC support for IBM AS/400".
However, it isn't clear if this relates only to the Edge Integration Cell or not.
Any clarification much appreciated.
Regards
Steve

entry.title: Re: how shipping pt. get capture in OBD via transfer posting
entry.content:
Hey Jagdeep,

Thanks for sharing the KBA note, this is really helpful, i checked at table T001W and there 0001 is defaulted, I believe this came from assignment of shipping point to plant.

Earlier I tried to delete this from assignment just to ensure what shipping pt will consider in OBD but in next OBD also it was considering as 0001 only. So now i'm quite confuse is this really capturing from shipping pt assignment to plant config or anything else is maintained.

I checked another config suggested by you (define default values for deliveries) and there another shp pt is defined. can you help me here to clear my confusion.

 Thanking you in advance.

entry.title: Premium Engagement Session "Security PE services for SAP Business Data Cloud" (Virtual, May 8, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual

May 8, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you this year’s third Premium Engagement Session focusing on the important topic “Security PE services for SAP Business Data Cloud”. This event is taking place virtually on May 8, 2025. Check out the detailed agenda here.

We continue these virtual Premium Engagements Sessions, as we can see many companies are very interested in this way of learning, networking, and sharing – after this May session, we have the next session 18 September covering AI & PE Services. Watch out for the invitation.

This year we will also have a Premium Engagement onsite event in Copenhagen on June 11th to 12th for all our PE customers in Nordic & Baltics – SAP MaxAttention, SAP ActiveAttention, and SAP Value Assurance. The owners of the PE engagement will hear more in the coming weeks, and we will have a mix of the latest from SAP combined with customer stories.

In this May event about PE & SAP Business Data Cloud, we will learn about what SAP Business Data Cloud is all about, what it can give your company and what services we have in PE which can help you getting started, including the security side of the BDC. We have several expert speakers, who will be sharing valuable insights and there will be opportunity for raising your questions.

Don't miss this fantastic chance to gain practical insights and network with like-minded professionals who are embarking on SAP Business Data Cloud. We invite you to join us and participate in this engaging discussion. Your presence would add great value to the event.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: Internal Server Error in SAP Build Work Zone: Seeking Resolution

entry.title: Copying Control from sales documents to billing documents in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
In this short blog post, I would like to demonstrate the process of setting up copying control of a custom field value from a sales order to a billing document in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Here are the steps involved to implement this:

1) Create the Custom Field in the desired business context. In my example, it will be sales document item, and I will proceed to enable the business scenario “Sales Document to Billing Document on Item Level”:

2) Implement the custom logic

    • In the “Custom Logic” app, create a new enhancement implementation for the Custom Logic Extension Point “SD_BIL_DATA_TRANSFER”
    • Add a filter value to identify your implementation: in my example Sales_To_Bill_Copy_Control
    • Implement the custom logic to transfer the field value:
*   Mandatory step for all implementations:
*   Copy the importing parameters to the changing parameters.
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc TO bil_doc_res.
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc_item TO bil_doc_item_res.
    MOVE-CORRESPONDING bil_doc_item_contr TO bil_doc_item_contr_res.


*   Example field manipulation of billing document item:
* Get custom field value from the reference sales order
 Select single YY1_PSPElement_SDI from I_SalesDocumentItem
    into @DATA(lv_psp_element)
    where salesdocument = @Bil_doc_item-REFERENCESDDOCUMENT and  salesdocumentitem = @Bil_doc_item-REFERENCESDDOCUMENTITEM.
* Writes the value into the billing document item custom field
    bil_doc_item_res-yy1_pspelement_bdi = lv_psp_element.

3) Configure the Data Transfer Routine

    • Access the configuration UI “Define Custom Routines for Data Transfer to Billing Documents” (SSCUI 102865)
    • Add a new entry for the data transfer routine and assign a Routine Number (between 3000000 and 3009999)

 

    • Assign the Enhancement ID (filter value) to the routine number

 

4) Set Up Copying Control

    • Access the configuration UI “Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Billing Document” (SSCUI 102762)
    • Configure the copying control settings for sales document to billing document. In my example, it is for the transfer of data from sales orders to invoices (OR -> F2) on header level. On item level, for service items (TAD) for which billing documents are directly created from the sales orders, assign the routine created in the previous step.

5) Test the Implementation

    • Create a sales order and enter a value in the custom field
    • Create a billing document based on the sales order
    • Verify that the custom field value is copied correctly to the billing document

With this blog post, you should have the tools necessary to setup the data transfer mechanisms involved to facilitate the copying control of custom field values from sales documents to billing documents. The process is similar by using the respective extension points and configuration activities when it comes to the copying control between sales orders, delivery documents and billing documents.

entry.title: Sap business One 2007
entry.content: How to import SQL database from SAP business one 2007 to sap business one 2010? Can anybody help?

entry.title: PT_COR_REQ Entry not updated in infotype 2011 and table TEVEN after Modifying fields in BADI
entry.content:
Hi Expert

I am in a situation where I need to modify the value of ORIGF or update the Value of TERID through BADI PT_COR_REQ while running T-code PTCOR. I have implemented the BADI and using method IF_EX_PT_COR_REQ~POST_VIA_BLOP.

Below is the code I have written to update the value of TERID the table is getting updated here but its not reflecting in Infotype 2011 or in Table TEVEN.

METHOD if_ex_pt_cor_req~post_via_blop.


    DATA: lt_event TYPE TABLE OF ptcor_tevdata_struc,
          ls_event LIKE LINE OF lt_event.

*    ex_tevent_tab = im_tevent_tab.
          lt_event = im_tevent_tab.

    LOOP AT lt_event INTO ls_event.
      ls_event-terid = 'BAC'.
*      MODIFY ex_tevent_tab FROM ls_event INDEX sy-tabix.
      MODIFY lt_event FROM ls_event INDEX sy-tabix.
    ENDLOOP.

    ex_tevent_tab[] = lt_event[].
  ENDMETHOD.

Post Method

Kindly Share your opinion that whether this BADI is suitable for this requirement or I'm doing anything wrong anything else here.  Aside this BADI any other option where I can update or modified the Fields Value that entry should be visible in Infotype 2011 and in Table TEVEN.

Thanks in Advance.

entry.title: Error While Marking Delivery Completion Indicator On PO
entry.content:
While attempting to mark the Delivery Completion Indicator on a Purchase Order, the system displays the following error message:

"Material is GR, cannot change Net Price."

It appears that the error is triggered despite no intentional changes being made to the net price. Requesting assistance in resolving this issue or clarification on the cause of this message.

entry.title: Re: Access to your data in S/4HANA Cloud Public edition a...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

When a customer moves from a 2-system to a 3-system landscape in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's essentially a reimplementation with a new contract. That means the current system (with the 2-system landscape) will eventually be shut down. Since the customer still needs access to their data for audits or legal reasons, there are a few options SAP provides.

 

What SAP offers for data access after system termination:

1. Data Export from SAP

 

Before the system is shut down, the customer can request a full data export. SAP provides this in a structured XML format, which is legally compliant and can be stored outside of SAP. This export has to be requested through a support ticket on the SAP Launchpad before the tenant is terminated.

2. Read-Only Access

 

SAP may allow the system to stay in read-only mode for a short period (usually up to 3 months) after contract termination. This gives the customer time to manually access, download, or review data. This should also be requested ahead of time through the customer’s SAP contact (Customer Engagement Executive or Customer Success Partner).

3. Store the Data Externally

 

If the customer needs to keep the data accessible in a user-friendly way (not just as raw XML), they can use third-party archiving solutions like OpenText, KGS, or Datavard. These tools can store the exported SAP data securely and make it easier to search or retrieve if needed later.

 

What the customer should do:

1. Get in touch with SAP ASAP to plan the data export and/or request read-only access.

2. Decide where and how they want to store the exported data – internally or using a third-party tool.

3. Make sure they request the export before the system contract ends.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Where can I learn make-to-stock (MTS) production processes in event-based Production Accounting?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Evaluating Production Accounting in Make-to-Stock Scenarios in SAP S/4HANA learning journey.

Overview
This essential learning journey covers various Make-to-Stock production processes from the perspective of event-based Production Accounting by Order and by Period. It equips consultants with the knowledge and skills necessary to effectively analyze production processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using different cost objects, such as production orders, process orders in joint production scenarios, and product cost collectors.

Learning objectives

  • Explore the MTS production process from a management accounting perspective
  • Identify relevant master data needed in Production Accounting by Order and by Period
  • Evaluate event-based postings to cost objects from different production steps 
  • Learn to analyze production costs, event-based Work-In-Process (WIP) and variance calculation.
  • Explain the error handling process

Prerequisites

  • Introducing Product Cost Planning and Production Accounting in SAP S/HANA

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Need APIs for Automating Finance Data Uploads (No Manual Entry)
entry.content: Hello, if I'm not mistaken this is a duplicate of https://community.sap.com/t5/financial-management-q-a/sap-s-4hana-public-cloud-need-apis-for-automating-finance-data-uploads-no/qaq-p/14082331. If this is is the case, can you please delete one of your questions to speed up answering of your questions? Thanks very much! Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not displayed on output
entry.content:
Dear Expert Community,

I have an Adobe output form (DEBIT MEMO) in S4Hana public cloud where I have added a field. The field is visible/invisible on the printout as expected, but the hardcoded value of the field remains empty / doesn't display. The caption of the field is correctly displayed (or not) as prompted by the conditions, proving that the active/inactive conditions work correctly. Just the value of the field doesn't get displayed.

The active/inactive condition of this field, as well as the hardcoded value, are controlled by a piece of java code as shown below (in the below, the real Partner number is replaced by XXXXXX):

<script contentType="application/x-javascript">if(this.parent.Partner.rawValue =="XXXXXX") {this.rawValue =="TEST"}; else
{
this.presence = "inactive";
}
</script>

What could be the reason and what could be done? 

Best regards,

Katja

entry.title: Question regarding Deep Links for List View in SAP Service Cloud Version 2
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have a requirement where we need a hyperlink in a analytical story to navigate to the list view of Cases in SAP Service Cloud Version 2 with specific filters, for example: List View of Cases with Status = 'Open'.

I understand that the deep link for the case view is: https://{tenantURL}/go/list/case  

Is there any way to add parameters to the link such that the list view is filtered? Please do provide your insights.

Thanks in advance,
Karthik C M.

I would like to know is there any possibility of changing the layouts to existing PO forms I got to know it can be done via adobe life cycle designer as I am new, I would like to explore more about it .is there any tutorial or links that can be referred to do some testing's by myself.

entry.title: Re: Error fiori app generator failed
entry.content:
If you're experiencing issues, please try the following steps:

  1. Ensure only one project folder is open at a time on the left panel — having too many open projects can cause loading issues.

  2. If it's still not working, check if your Dev Space is full — this can prevent the app from functioning properly.

  3. As a final step, try opening the application in Incognito Mode to rule out any browser cache or extension conflicts.

Let me know if the issue persists after these steps!

entry.title: SAP for Me "You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation"
entry.content:
Hi,

While performing an upgrade we ran into an issue where there seems to be an error on SAP for me with regards to our installation number. SAP for me reports back that we do not have a maintenance contract for the installation.  "You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation. Details in KBA " 
License Keys application shows message: You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation - SAP for Me - SAP for Me  we had a look and indeed the installation reports expired on the maintenance agreement but we can't create a temporary maintenance entitlement which is urgently needed for the upgrade to continue and meet the planning with the business. 

Please have a look at the installation number because as we see, we should have a valid contract. 

Regards,









------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP for Me "You do not have a maintenance contract fo...
entry.content:
Dear valued customer,

I understand you are located under Service & Support at License key application. While selecting the installation to extend your key you get the error "You do not have a maintenance contract for this installation". You also might have realized the "Details in KBA " beside that error. By clicking on it you will always find the information on the next steps for such errors. 

In that case I would need you to contact the SAP Contract Department or your responsible SAP Account Representative. Seems like the maintenance frame of the installation needs to be reviewed and corrected.

Following two KBAs helps you guide to that channel.

https://me.sap.com/notes/2626344

https://me.sap.com/notes/1660069

I would love being a help but unfortunately the SAP for Me technical Support Team does not have any access rights to maintain contract data.

Best wishes Dragana

SAP Support SAP for Me.

entry.title: Real-Time Configuration Progress Tracker in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
While exploring SAP Learning Hub, I noticed a powerful yet simple UX feature — real-time tracking of progress. It shows learners how many topics they’ve completed, how many are left, and even displays a clean progress bar. This got me thinking…

Why don’t we have something similar inside SAP SuccessFactors?

As consultants, partners, and administrators, we often work across modules like:

  • Compensation

  • Performance and Goals (PMGM)

  • Employee Central (EC)

  • Onboarding, and more…

But there’s no built-in progress tracker to visually reflect how much of the configuration or project setup is completed during implementation or maintenance. Imagine if we could see a simple status like:

6 of 10 key configuration items completed
Progress: 60%
Pending: Eligibility rules, Route Map, EC Integration

if this could be done then it will reduce multiple clicks and improve SAP ecosystem

Benefits of a Real-Time Configuration Progress Tracker in SAP SuccessFactors

 Benefit Area  Description
Visibility & TransparencyClearly shows what’s configured vs. pending. No more scattered status updates.
Implementation EfficiencyKeeps consultants, admins, and PMs aligned. Faster go-lives, fewer blockers.
Customer ConfidenceClients can monitor progress visually in real time. Builds trust and clarity.
Agile EnablementSupports iterative delivery, sprint-based rollouts, and milestone tracking.
Error ReductionStandardized checklists prevent missing key setup steps.
Post-Go-Live UseUseful for ongoing maintenance, updates, and change requests.
Team CollaborationAll module owners see the same live status. Improves cross-functional flow.
GamificationAdd motivation through visual progress bars and milestone badges.
UX AlignmentMatches SAP Learning Hub’s intuitive learner UX. User-first for admins too.

 

Suggest me your ideas as well, i am new to the role and exploring SAP successfactor.

entry.title: Re: Production order GR .Document XXXXXXX does not contain any selectable items Message No M7064

Hi

Can you please share the screenshot of the Production Order Main screen, then GR Tab in Production Order and while doing GR what exactly error you are facing. Let me know

entry.title: Re: Value Help with Additional Binding in RAP
entry.content: Thank you for sharing the blog, it was useful.

entry.title: Credit Limit Error When Creating Sales Order for Extended Sales Org
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a scenario related to SAP Credit Management and would appreciate your guidance.

I have a customer initially created under Sales Organization 2000 with Credit Segment 2000. I assigned a credit limit of 1000, and everything works fine — for example:

  • If I create a sales order of 900, it's saved successfully.

  • If I try to create an order of 1100, the system blocks it due to exceeding the credit limit, which is expected and working as intended.

Now, I extended the same customer to another Sales Organization 4000, and assigned Credit Segment 4000 with a credit limit of 5000.

However, when I try to create a sales order of 1600 in Sales Org 4000, the system throws an error saying that the credit limit is exceeded — even though the credit limit under Credit Segment 4000 is set to 5000.

Can anyone help me understand why this is happening? Am I missing some configuration or master data dependency that is causing the system to still consider the old credit segment or combine limits?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Faraz Ali.

entry.title: Re: TABSTRIP PROBLEM
entry.content:
The syntax error message says that you have a screen element that doesn't have a name. So... if you navigate to the element list, and look at line 8 of that list, you're bound to see a screen element that is missing the name.

If this doesn't help you solve it by yourself, a screenshot of the element list would be helpful.

entry.title: Re: Function module in rap
entry.content: Thanks for sharing the blog, it was useful.

entry.title: Re: how to set a filter "field = empty " in FIORI App man...
entry.content: check the table property we have filter propery directly bind in items= use it

entry.title: Re: IDEA integration for Turkey Incoming invoices
entry.content: Hello Martin, Is this Solution based on SAP DRC FW Add-On or it´s pure SNI Solution utilizing eDocument table? Can you confirm this?

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to configure and manage emission management processes within SAP EHS?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Configuring, Managing, and Integrating Emission Processes in SAP learning journey.

Overview
Learn how to configure and manage emission management processes within SAP EHS, including setting up master data, monitoring and tracking emission data, and ensuring compliance with environmental regulations and standards. Additionally, gain the skills to integrate SAP EHS Management, environment management data with SAP Reporting and Analytics solutions, such as SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Sustainability Control Tower.

Learning objectives

  • Comprehend the Emission Management Process in depth. 
  • Integrate SAP EHS Management, environment management with Reporting and Analytics Solutions. 
  • Prepare and Manage Master Data. 

Please post you question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 
Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm not that fit in Javascript via Adobe Forms, but it seems suspicious that you have two '=' signs. Maybe you can try = 'TEST' instead of == 'TEST'.

Does your logic even reach the If statement? There are many ways to fetch the parent values. Maybe you have to change that part. There is a way to fetch it via strings.. maybe that works.

Hope that helps

entry.title: Re: TABSTRIP PROBLEM
entry.content: You are right, I fixed it. Thank you

entry.title: Re: S4Cloud: Adobe Form - custom field hardcoded text not...
entry.content: Hi Fabienne, Thanks for the suggestion! The double "=" -signs are as instructed by Java syntax, and they are successfully used that way in numerous other places in our output forms. Sometimes, I have seen even === recommended, but I usually stick to ==. I do think the if-statement is reached because I can see the caption displayed if the condition Partner = XXXXXX is met, and - converserly - not displayed if this condition isn't met. Also, I should add that the field length for the value is 20 characters and the value itself is less. The other properties of the field are identical with other fields that do work correctly.

entry.title: Changing Quota value in a Quota arrangement not applying new percentages correctly.
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am experiencing an issue where a change is made to the Quota values in a quota arrangement (via MEQ1) but the planned order creation after the change does not appear to work correctly.  The problem appears that some residual quantity remains in the Allocated quantity field and this is then redistributed when MRP reads the quota arrangement.

Example

Product A has a quota arrangement with a Quota of 50 for production version ABCD and a Quota of 50 for production version ZYXW.

There are demands for 100,000 units in the system

The allocated quantity for each PV in the Quota is 75,000 units.

So there is a residual 50,000 units (2 x 75,000 - 100,000) in the allocated quantity.

When MRP is run the planned orders are evenly split between the two production versions.

If I now change the Quota to be 80 for ABCD and 20 for ZYXW and run MRP. 

The residual quantity appears to still be applied so the planned orders are initially all for production version ABCD, then after the system equalises itself they go to an 80/20 ratio.  It appears that as the historical distribution of the PV's was 50/50 the system now uses ABCD 100% until an 80/20 ratio for the whole horizon is reached.

I have tried MEQB to process the revision.  I have also tried Edit >  Base Quantities > Collective Calculation and Edit > Reset   (in MEQ1) to try and have the new quota percentage applied immediately irrespective on the history.

The only way I can make the new quota arrangement percentages to be applied to current and future demands is to delete the quota arrangement with MEQ7 and then creating a new one.  Am I missing something?  Can anyone explain where the residual quantity in the quota arrangement comes from and why it would be redistributed upon an update?

Thanks in Advance,

Jez

entry.title: Re: Error While Marking Delivery Completion Indicator On PO
entry.content: Please share the screen shot of the error with message number

entry.title: Using a Function Module in SAP RAP managed
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I'm trying my best to use the FM BAPI_USER_CREATE1 so i'll be able to create a user on S/4, since i'm working in a managed scenario i have implemented a determination on save so when a new record is created a method that is using BAPI_USER_CREATE1 will be triggered as shown below

determination createUser on save {create;}

 

METHOD CreateUser.
  READ ENTITIES OF zi_user1_rh IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY User
    ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(entites).

    LOOP AT entites INTO DATA(entity).
      DATA: ls_address TYPE bapiaddr3.
      ls_address-firstname = entity-Firstname.
      ls_address-lastname = entity-Lastname.
      DATA: ls_logondata TYPE bapilogond.
      ls_logondata-ustyp = 'A'.
      DATA: lv_password TYPE bapipwd VALUE '27456520Recon..',
            lv_username TYPE bapibname-bapibname.
      lv_username = entity-Username.

    ENDLOOP.
    CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_USER_CREATE1'
      IN UPDATE TASK
      EXPORTING
        username  = lv_username
        logondata = ls_logondata
        password  = lv_password
        address   = ls_address.

  ENDMETHOD.

PS: Please ignore the password that is being mentioned directly in the code as i'm still testing 
Unfortunately that didn't work and i got the below st22 dump.

So i decided to take a different approach, so i create a different method which the same logic as the previous one and made it executable via an action button as shown below

action CreateUser result[1] $self;

However i'm still getting exactly the same dump, i came across many blogs like the below one which indicates that a validation or a determination should work with a FM in a managed scenario.
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/adding-custom-code-function-module-in-managed-scenario-sap-abap-rap/qaq-p/12617692/comment-id/4732597#M4732597 
And recommendations please ?

entry.title: Need Help Creating Over-Delivery % Report Based on PO in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
I’m working on a custom report in SAP Business ByDesign to identify Purchase Orders (POs) where the delivered quantity exceeds the ordered quantity. I’d like to filter the report to show only those PO numbers with over-delivery and also calculate the percentage of over-delivery.

Here’s what I’ve done so far:

Data Source Used:

  • Purchase Order Item Details

     

    Goal:

    1. Filter only those POs where Delivered Qty > Ordered Qty

    2. Show a % over-delivery calculated as:

       

       
      CopyEdit
      (Delivered Qty - Ordered Qty) / Ordered Qty * 100
       

      🧪 What I Tried:

      • Created a custom Key Figure for (Delivered - Ordered Qty) → Works fine.

      • Created another Key Figure for Division, then Multiplication by 100.

      • But I couldn't get the percentage to display in percentage format—values appear as decimal numbers instead.

      • Also, I couldn’t filter the report to only show POs with over-delivery using that formula.

        🆘Where I’m Stuck:

        1. How can I apply a filter so that I only see POs where delivered quantity is greater than ordered quantity?

        2. Is there a way to get the over-delivery % to show correctly in percentage format, not just as decimal values?

          ✅Any help would be appreciated!

          If this isn't fully achievable using a calculated key figure, maybe there’s an alternative approach (e.g., using a different data source or a workaround)?

          Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: how to set a filter "field = empty " in FIORI App man...
entry.content: You need to first specify you are asking about sapui5 fiori application filter or backend filter

entry.title: Attribute text not showing after deploying analytical model in Datasphere
entry.content:
We have an analytical model in Datasphere that includes the "Employee" field, which was correctly displaying its corresponding text—until the model was deployed. Below, I explain in detail how it was configured and at what point it stopped working, since no changes were made that could explain why the text suddenly stopped appearing.

 

How was it configured when it was working?

The source view of the analytical model includes both an attribute association (EMPLOYEE_ATTR_1) and a text association (EMPLOYEE_TEXT) for the "Employee" field.

In the attribute list of the view, only the attribute association is assigned—not the text one (only one can be assigned):

We could see the "T" icon in the source view next to the "Employee" field, indicating that text was correctly linked:

Additionally, the EMPLOYEE_ATTR_1 table has two associations: one to another attribute table (EMPLOYEE_ATTR_2) and another to EMPLOYEE_TEXT. As in the previous case, only one association can be assigned to the "Employee" field, and the one assigned is EMPLOYEE_ATTR_2: 

In the analytical model, the "Employee" field is marked to allow the use of attributes:

I can’t confirm if the "T" icon ever showed up for the "Employee" field inside the analytical model itself, but the text display was working correctly with this configuration.

 

What change caused the issue?

Nothing in the configuration was changed. However, these steps were taken:

  • A deploy was performed on the EMPLOYEE_ATTR_1 attribute table.

  • A deploy was then performed on the analytical model (From this point onward, the "Employee" text stopped being displayed).

We also noticed that in other analytical models using the same setup and referencing EMPLOYEE_ATTR_1, the "Employee" text also stops displaying after deployment—even if no actual changes are made.
So, with each deploy, the text disappears from the "Employee" field.

(Language settings were not changed.)

 

What workaround did we implement?

First, we tried reverting to previous versions of EMPLOYEE_ATTR_1 and the analytical model, but this didn’t restore the text.

So we tried a different approach:
The second employee attribute table (EMPLOYEE_ATTR_2) does have a text association, and this one can be assigned to the "Employee" field:

In the analytical model, we activated the attributes from EMPLOYEE_ATTR_2:

Now we see the "T" icon:

And in our SAC dashboard, instead of using the original "Employee" field, we use the one from EMPLOYEE_ATTR_2, which has the text association.
In other words, we had to add an additional field just to display the text.

I wonder if this is actually the correct solution, and whether we need to apply the same changes in other models and dashboards in order to display the texts.

 

Final questions: 

  • Is there no way to use a single field that has both an attribute association and can also display the text?
  • Has anyone experienced the same issue? (The setup used to work as described, and suddenly stopped working without an apparent reason.)
  • What’s the recommended solution to display texts for fields that also have attribute associations—especially when there are multiple levels of attributes?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: Timestamp in SRT_MONI Reflecting When Message Was Fir...
entry.content:
Hello Dominik,

I hope you're doing well.

Could you please clarify what you mean by "Execution Date & Time"? Are you referring to the technical fields CHANGED_DATE and CHANGED_TIME in SRT_MONI?

As part of my analysis of the data exchange between the S/4 system (SRT_MONI) and CPI, I’ve observed that the CHANGE_TS field in SRT_MONI generally appears after the log_start time of the corresponding CPI message. This leads to a paradox where the message seems to be registered in CPI before it was actually sent from SRT_MONI.

Therefore, I would like to ask: Which field accurately indicates the outbound timestamp of a message leaving SRT_MONI?

In the attached screenshot, I’ve highlighted in red the same tracked object IDs (Sales Order ID + Transaction ID), first as seen in S/4 (top) and then in CPI (bottom). In blue, I’ve highlighted the timestamps showing this paradox:

  • 2025-04-22T12:48:47.810Z → First step: S/4 – Message delivered

  • 2025-04-22T10:48:47.781Z → Second step: CPI – Message processing started

Basically CPI as seen the message from S4 0,03 seconds (30ms) before change_ts value written in SRT_MONI.

Looking forward to your insights on this.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: There is a difference in count of SPAU notes in SPDD...
entry.content:
Hi,

There could be many reasons for that. I think if they are obsolete you can confirm them. There is also a batch job that confirms all obsolete notes. I see no problem with that.

Hope that helps.

entry.title: Re: PO conditions payment terms
entry.content:
Hi ,

There may be a configuration or rule that allows the system to prioritize the vendor's payment terms.
You can set logic here to   Prefer PO payment terms 
Fall back to vendor payment terms  Or
use condition-based logic (e.g., based on vendor group, document type, etc.).

Thanks,
SK.

entry.title: SAC Planning on Future date display issue
entry.content:
Hi All Need your Inputs   SAC Created Planning Report ,

our requirement is in Report  based on GL Account  , Actual and Planning (Budget)  data to be displayed Right now data is available for 2023 .

when user select in input Control filer (Year 2024)  report should display Previous year and current Year Actual and Budget 

To Tried different solution but no luck 

Solution -1  created   Input control filter for version and Time   we enabled all version selected 2023 and 2024 as there is no data available for 2024 and budget its not displaying as user need  Budget data based on previous year 

 

Solution 2 : we created Two input controls for Time and version 

Input control 1 (Time select 2023) Input control 2 (Time select 2024)  its not working 

Input control 2 (Version select Actual ) Input control 2 (Version  select Budget ) its not working 

 

Can you suggest best  way to get Future data based on year

entry.title: Down Payment in Purchase Orders - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
What impact does the functionality of the down payment fields in the Purchase Order (header and item) in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud have on accounting processes? 

How do the accounting processes differ depending on whether down payment type M (Mandatory Down Payment), N (No Down Payment) or V (Voluntary Down Payment) is selected?

entry.title: Switching ALV on a dynpro container -> old ALV remains visible
entry.content:
Hello, I have a dynpro with a custom container on it. I consecutively want to display two different ALV grids on this. The flow is like this:

  • call the screen
  • instantiate a container object on the screen container (PBO)
  • instantiate the first alv grid with the container object
  • display the first ALV
  • After the user leaves the display:
  • leave the screen
  • free the container object
  • call the screen again
  • instantiate a new container object on the screen
  • instantiate the second alv grid with the (new) container object
  • display the second ALV

As a result, the first ALV shows up again on my screen where I expected to see the second ALV. What am I missing?

entry.title: Outlook Email Based Approve/Reject Workflow for Purchase Order PR in ECC
entry.content:
SAP approval process from external mails

Scenario : For Purchase order / PR approval

Standard SAP process : When purchase order created it will trigger workflow and through mail notification it will send to the SAP inbox, it can be approved/rejected.

Requirement : In the same way external mail, should have option to approve or reject So that this can update backend ECC SAP PO directly. Without login to SAP users want to approve or reject PO.

Thanks in advance

 

Pranav

entry.title: Re: Down Payment in Purchase Orders - SAP S/4HANA Public...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the down payment fields in the purchase order (both header and item level) have a direct impact on how accounting handles supplier payments and invoice processing.

You can choose between three down payment categories: M (Mandatory), V (Voluntary), and N (None). Each one changes how the PO behaves from both a process and accounting point of view.

 

M - Mandatory Down Payment

If you set the PO to “M”, you’re telling the system that a down payment is required before the supplier’s invoice can be posted.

  • The accounting team will need to post a down payment first (using the “Manage Supplier Down Payments” app, for example).
  • Only once that’s done, the invoice can go through.
  • When the invoice is posted, the system automatically clears the down payment against the invoice amount.

This setup is usually used for high-value or capital-intensive purchases, where you want to secure commitment before processing the invoice.

 

V - Voluntary Down Payment

This option gives you more flexibility. You can make a down payment, but it’s not required.

  • The invoice can be posted with or without a down payment being made.
  • If a down payment is made, it’ll still be cleared automatically during invoice posting—same as with type M.
  • If not, the invoice just posts as normal.

It’s useful when down payments are based on supplier negotiations or aren't always known in advance.

 

N - No Down Payment

Pretty straightforward: no down payment expected or involved.

  • The PO will follow the standard procurement and accounting process—goods receipt, then invoice receipt, then payment.
  • There’s no down payment posting, and nothing to clear.

 

In short:

  • Use M when you want financial control and need to ensure payment happens upfront.
  • Use V for flexible cases where a down payment might happen.
  • Use N when you’re just doing a regular purchase with no prepayment.

 

I hope this will help answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

Note : GenAI was used to help generate this content

entry.title: Re: Trend Micro Deep Security reports HEU_AEGIS_CRYPT err...
entry.content:
I figured out the reason that Trend Micro was flagging my new application as malware with the HEU_AEGIS_CRYPT event. Apparently this only seems to happen with a console application and not with a Windows GUI application. 

The default configuration for DI API involves generating log files under the %ProgramData%\SAP\SAP Business One\Log\SAP Business One\ directory. DI API starts writing these log files when you open a connection to an SAP company database. Apparently enough of these files are generated to convince Trend Micro’s behavior detection that you’re running some sort of ransomware.

I temporarily disabled DI API logging on the server where I had the new application running using the instructions in SAP Note 3406982. I backed up the b1LogConfig.xml file in the C:\Program Files\SAP\SAP Business One DI API\Conf directory and replaced it with the contents described in that note. 

If Trend Micro can be configured to exclude the %ProgramData%\SAP\SAP Business One\Log\SAP Business One\ directory, then Trend Micro shouldn’t be shutting down this application. 

The client has now added an exclusion for this directory.

entry.title: Which values can be used for valuating goods receipt from a production order?
entry.content:
Hello!

Which values can be used for valuating goods receipt from a production order?
There is no material cost estimate and no sales order costing available. The value for the goods receipt is taken from the planned cost estimate of the production order.
Is there a configuration option—similar to the valuation variant for material withdrawals—where the system checks available values in a defined sequence? What does it depends on? 

 

Thank you and kind regards

Olga

entry.title: Why message displayed shows no text, only the variable 1? (BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO)
entry.content:
I implemented the BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO to do custom checks in ME22N and F0842A (purchase order).

I fill the return parameter MESSAGES with message 001 in message class ZPO ("there is an error concerning item &1") with variable 1 "00020" but the displayed error message is "00020" alone.

I expect "there is an error concerning item 00020"

Note that the message exists in English language, and ABAP code running in English.

MESSAGE e001(zpo) WITH '00020' INTO DATA(lv_message).
messages = VALUE #( ( messageid        = sy-msgid
                      messagetype      = sy-msgty
                      messagenumber    = sy-msgno
                      messagevariable1 = sy-msgv1
                      messagevariable2 = sy-msgv2
                      messagevariable3 = sy-msgv3
                      messagevariable4 = sy-msgv4 ) ).

What's the problem with the code?

Thanks.

Sandra

System: S/4HANA 2023 FPS 01 / On premises.

entry.title: Re: Batch managing in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hi @MarinaLevy ,

Are you having any issues with assigning or tracking batches?

If both component and final product are controlling batch, you should be prompted to select which ones you are drawing from and creating in the warehouse, respectively.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web)
entry.content:
Consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises. We have identified areas directly relevant to users and plan to introduce a number of design updates in SAP Fiori for web across our suite of products to further increase consistency. We are confident that they will all improve user satisfaction – but take a look for yourselves!

The areas we have redesigned and which we plan to provide across our suite of products this year and next year are:

  • Shell bar (at the top of the screen)
  • Navigation menu: side navigation
  • User menu
  • Notifications
  • Search in the shell bar

All these planned improvements are all very intuitive. Nevertheless, you might want to inform your users about these changes prior to an upgrade.

A definite highlight for users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA is the planned new side navigation menu, which gives users direct access to their favorite apps from wherever they are – without having to first go to My Home to find them. This is currently planned to become available in the releases in the second half of 2025.

This post consists entirely of forward-looking statements, for which our usual legal disclaimers apply: our plans are subject to change without notice. The features are planned to appear in stages, so don’t expect all of them necessarily to come in the same release. Also, the design previews you see here are not all 100% final, so expect some minor changes before they get released in products.

 

Shell Bar

The shell bar appears at the top of every screen of every SAP application, and already looks very similar across our products. Now we are going one step further by specifying it in such a way that it follows an identical design, and ensuring this with our UI technology SAPUI5 by providing a web component for use by our products in implementing the design. For example, we plan to replace the existing implementation of the shell bar in the SAP Fiori launchpad with the new web component seamlessly embedded in SAPUI5.

Figure 1 shows the target design. In the examples in the next section “navigation menu” you can see a preview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud running in the SAP Fiori launchpad (Figures 7 and 8).

The target design provides:

  • A consistent product representation across our suite of products by having a dedicated place for the product identifier.
  • Guidance for providing standard patterns for the part in the middle – for example as shown in Figure 1 indicating that you are in a trial system with 30 days remaining, or in Figure 8 indicating the name of the application in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA.
  • Stable positioning of shell actions such as opening Joule, Notifications, Help and User Menu.

The design also gives you a sneak preview of a planned product-switcher icon, which would allow users to switch directly to other products, and which would probably come later than the new shell bar itself. Similarly the “Trial” indicator may come with late releases.

Figure 1: Target design for the updated shell bar. Some of the features shown may come in later releases than others. Alt Text: Image shows only the shell bar, with the menu icon on the left, followed by the SAP logo, text “Product Identifier”, a vertical separator line, text “Trial” in a mauve container and text “30 days remaining”. On the far right an icon with 3x3 dots for the planned product switcher, then going left: a user avatar, a help icon, a notifications icon with a red indicator showing unread notifications, the Joule icon and a search field.

Navigation Menu: Side Navigation

Many SAP products already offer a navigation menu on the left of the screen. With today’s wide screens on desktops and laptops, this is a good use of screen space, and also a pattern already familiar to many people. Hence we have decided to apply that approach to navigation across the SAP Business Suite in upcoming releases.

The new design offers three variants, as depicted in Figure 2. In all cases, the navigation menu appears on the left of the screen. The menu can be always fully visible (“Expanded”), or it can be collapsed to leave more space for the actual application UI (“Collapsed”), or it can appear as an overlay, after the user has clicked on the menu icon (“Overlay Mode”).

The menu can have a hierarchy of entries. In Figure 2, the “Favorites” section has been expanded to show two items below it, each labelled “Favorite item” in the example.

Figure 2: Side navigation variants. Alt Text: three variants shown for a vertical list of navigation targets: Expanded, Collapsed and Overlay Mode: The collapsed mode shows only icons, Expanded and Overlay Mode show names next to each item. At the top, the list starts with “Home” and “Favorites”. Below that that a “Navigation Group” which has been expanded to show five “Nav Item” entries, one of which is expanded to show two “Child Item” entries. At the very bottom, separated by vertical white space from the navigation targets, two quick actions are displayed (“Quick Create” and “New Item”).

Figures 3, 4 and 5 show design mock-ups of an imaginary application listing sales orders using the patterns “Expanded”, “Collapsed” and “Overlay Mode” respectively.

Figure 3: Side navigation: example of the “Expanded” pattern. Alt Text: The expanded pattern on the left of the screen. The majority of the screen to the right shows a tabular list of sales orders.

Figure 4: Side navigation: example of the “Collapsed” pattern. Alt Text: The collapsed pattern on the left of the screen, which shows a tabular list of sales orders.

Figure 5: Side navigation: example of the “Overlay Mode” pattern. Alt Text: The overlay mode pattern on the left of the screen, covering the first two columns of the tabular list of sales orders.

Side Navigation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA

Figures 6, 7 and 8 give you a preview of what this would look like for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA (on-prem) later this year (Q3/2025 and Q4/2025 respectively) – as you can see, we have opted for the “Overlay Mode” pattern. Note that some minor details of the design are likely to change before being released.

Figure 6 shows the initial menu, as it would look when a user clicks on the menu icon the first time. What is new compared to the current navigation menu is the section “Favorite Apps” which contains the user’s favorite apps, which they have personalized on the My Home page. As before, the menu provides direct access to a user’s Spaces and Pages.

Figure 6: Planned side navigation for SAP S4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA: Initial menu. Note that the design is subject to minor changes before being released. Alt Text: The image shows the top left of the My Home screen with the menu opened in overlay mode. The list starts with the entry “My Home”, followed by “Favorite Apps” with an “>” indicator that it is not expanded, “My Spaces” which has been expanded and shows entries “Billing”, “Sales Management”, “Price Management”, “Order Fulfillment Managem…”, “Order Fulfillment Execution”. “Price Management” and “Order Fulfillment Execution” are single entries, the others have ”>”  indicators that they have not been expanded. At the bottom of the overlay, below a horizontal separator line: “All Spaces” can be chosen.

Figure 7 shows the “Favorite Apps” expanded while on the My Home page. You can see that the list of favorite apps corresponds to the apps in the Favorites section of My Home (the section directly below the navigation menu). The user has defined two folders in the Favorites section (“Sales” and “Billing”); these are also shown as folders in the navigation menu. Similarly, the “My Spaces” section of the navigation menu lists the spaces which the user has pinned to the top of the My Home page.

Figure 7: Planned shell bar and side navigation for SAP S4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA: Favorites expanded. Note that the design is subject to minor changes before being released. Alt Text: The image shows the whole My Home page, with the navigation menu on the left, showing the Favorites expanded, containing a list of eight apps followed by two groups “Sales” and “Billing”. These have the same names as the two groups and eight apps shown in the My Home page, in the same order (from left to right in My Home).

The main advantage of the new side navigation for users is the fact that they can access their favorite apps as well as their spaces and pages directly from any application, without first having to navigate to the My Home page. Figure 8 shows what this would look like, using the “Manage Sales Orders – Version 2” application as an example.

Figure 8: Planned Side Navigation for SAP S4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA: available from within every application. Note that the design is subject to minor changes before being released. Alt Text: Image shows the top left of the screen, showing the same navigation menu as in Figure 7, but overlayed above the “Manage Sales Order – Version 2” app, which shows a list with only one entry.

User Menu

The user menu, which appears when clicking on the user’s avatar icon at the top right of the screen, has also been redesigned. Figure 9 shows the target design, which gives more space to show the user’s photo, if one is maintained. The design specifies that the settings should always appear at the top of the list, followed by the product-specific menu items. The sign-out option is now more clearly visible in its own separate section.

Figure 10 shows what the new design is planned to look like for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA later this year, comparing it with the current user menu.

Figure 9: Target design for updated user menu. Alt Text: Two images of an overlay are shown. The image on the left, below a heading “Simple”, shows a centered circular photo of the user, with their name, e-mail address and role below it. Below that, left aligned, three items are listed, each first with an icon and then the name “Settings”, “Legal Information”, “About”. At the bottom, below a horizontal separator bar, right aligned in blue an icon and text “Sign Out”. The image on the right, below a heading “With Other Accounts”, is identical to the one on the left except for a “Manage Account” button below the user’s role, and below that an item “Other Accounts (3)” followed  by a an edit icon. There is an expand indicator “>” to the left of the “Other Accounts (3)” text.

Figure 10: Current and planned user menu for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP S/4HANA. Note that the “Adapt UI” entry is not included on the right since the example user does not have the necessary key user authorization. Alt Text: Two images are shown, each only showing the top right of the screen with the user menu pop-over under the user avatar in the shell bar. On the left the current user menu is shown, on the right the planned menu, which is larger, and starts with a user avatar followed by the user name and e-mail address, and then the menu items left-aligned. At the bottom, below a horizontal separator bar, an icon and “Sign Out” text is shown, right-aligned.

Notifications

The new target design for notifications standardizes how notifications are to be displayed across the SAP Business Suite in the future, for applications as well as for central services such as SAP Build Work Zone. Figure 11 shows the target design, which shows what notifications from a central notification service could look like, with notifications from various products. Users will be able to sort the list: by date or by importance. As you can see in figure 11, the results in the list are grouped, e.g. by today, yesterday, last week and so on. Clicking on the settings icon takes you directly to the notifications section in the user settings dialog.

Figure 11: Target design for notifications. Alt Text: The image shows the top right of the screen, with the notification pop-over below the shell bar notification icon. It shows two expanded groups “Today” and “Yesterday”, each showing two items with header in bold and two lines of text. Below the text a line indicating the source product and application along with a date or time. The first item has an additional line containing a red “Important” indicator. The header of the pop-over has “Clear All” text, a sort icon and a settings icon on the very right.

Search in the Shell Bar

Finally, we are also planning to standardize the design of search in the shell bar, as you can see in Figure 12. The most relevant items should appear at the top of the suggest list, followed by groups of further search hits.

 

Figure 12: Target design for search in the shell bar. Alt Text: The image shows the shell header bar with the text “John Walters” entered in the search field, below which a drop-down menu lists five hits (“John Walters”, “John’s Digital Services”,…), followed by a section “Sales Orders” followed by two entries (“15665566 – Media John Productions”,…) and a section “Sales Quotes” (“46465464 –John’s Ventures”,…). At the very bottom, below a horizontal separator bar, the text in blue “Show All search Results [N]”.

Final words

I hope you are looking forward to seeing these design updates in our products as much as I am!

In case you missed my recent series of blog posts about our recent UX innovations, do have a look, it starts with this one:

SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More)

Particular highlights in the series are the great uses of GenAI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and in SAP SuccessFactors, which are covered in parts 2 and 3. But you’ll also learn about the new Home Page in Fieldglass, new applications and innovations in Ariba and SAP CX, SAC and BTP with Horizon, plus UI design and technology updates. Here is the full list of the posts with links:

  1. Central UX Services (the first post)
    • Joule – latest UX innovations
    • Central entry points and services:
      • SAP Start / SAP Build Work Zone
      • SAP Task Center
      • SAP Mobile Start
  2. ERP: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).
  3. HR: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More).
  4. Spend: SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass.
  5. Selling: SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail.
  6. Analytics and Platform: SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.
  7. UI Design and Technology for web and mobile.

entry.title: Re: Why message is limited to 50 characters in BAdI BD_MM...
entry.content:
It's a weird behavior but "normal" as documented in the BAdI definition.

This can also be seen in the code of the BAdI invocation and message MEPO 250:

Code:

" IMG documentation of BAdI BD_MMPUR_FINAL_CHECK_PO:
"   Parameter MESSAGES
"   This BAdI currently does not support the full scope of message handling.
"   Therefore, you can only use the attributes MESSAGETYPE and MESSAGEVARIABLE1 of the parameter.
"   The messages contain the following information:
"   - MESSAGETYPE
"     This key field contains one of the following values:◦A: Abort
"     - E: Error
"     - W: Warning
"     - I: Information
"   - MESSAGEVARIABLE1
"     This parameter contains the message text.
"     Maximum 50 characters.
MESSAGE e001(zpo) WITH '00020' INTO ls_message-messagevariable1.
ls_message-messagetype = 'E'.
APPEND ls_message TO messages.

entry.title: Re: purchase order formsediting or making changes
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Yes, you can definitely change the layout of existing PO forms in SAP using Adobe LiveCycle Designer. It’s the tool SAP uses for designing form templates like POs, invoices, delivery notes, etc.

 

To do so, you will need :

  • Adobe LiveCycle Designer (you install this on your PC, comes from SAP)
  • Access to your SAP system - either On-Premise (via SAP GUI) or S/4HANA Cloud
  • A bit of patience - it’s powerful but not super intuitive at first 😊

 

If you are working on S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition):

  1. Go to the “Maintain Form Templates” app in Fiori
  2. Search for the form: for POs, it’s usually MM_PUR_PURCHASE_ORDER
  3. Click on it  then copy it (you can’t edit the standard one directly)
  4. Download your custom version (you’ll get an file at the XDP format)
  5. Open that file in Adobe LiveCycle Designer on your PC
  6. Make changes: move stuff around, change logos, resize sections, etc
  7. Save it and upload it back into SAP
  8. Use the “Output Parameter Determination” app to assign your form to the PO output type

 

If you're on SAP GUI (On-Premise):

  1. Use transaction code SFP.
  2. Enter the form name, click "Layout" → that opens LiveCycle Designer.
  3. Do your editing and save it.
  4. Test it with FP_TEST_00.

 

Here’s things you can do on your layout :

  • Swap out the logo
  • Adjust the table that shows line items
  • Change fonts, add text, rearrange sections
  • Add custom fields (if needed – might need to expose them via CDS or context)

 

Regarding the tutos, you can maybe find your happiness in the SAP Help Portal or even on Youtube videos.

 

I hope this will help answer you with exploring the Adobe Livecycle Designer tool.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Preferred Unit of Measure in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a question regarding the Preferred Unit of Measure in EWM on SAP Public Cloud. In integration with production, we need the preferred unit of measure for certain products to be cartons. The goods receipt from production is done using the "Enter goods receipt for handling unit" application.

Here are the steps we have taken:

  1. In the warehouse monitor, we set the preferred unit of measure to CAR (Carton) for the product attribute.
  2. We then went to the "Enter goods receipt for handling unit" application and received the handling units from production.

However, when the goods were received in the warehouse monitor, the alternative unit of measure remained in pieces.

We found some articles about Preferred unit of measure working with inbound delivery but did not found how to make goods receipt from production with preferred unit of measure.

Is there a solution for the public cloud that would allow us to set the preferred unit of measure from production?

Thank you.

entry.title: New company code as a copy of an old one
entry.content:
Hello, I have been trying to create a new company code, using an old one as a template. Any way that I can copy the company details + pricing conditions and all facilities to a new one? We are moving to a new currency, Bulgaria changes to Euro and we need to set up our systems good. 


Thank you for your answer,
Nikola Nikolov

entry.title: Re: How to enable Certificate based Authentication with S...
entry.content: For SAP Commerce cloud inbound client certificate authentication, Can you please let us know how to generate certificates with private key specific to user connecting to commerce webservice calls. Is client certificate authentication is supported in Sap commerce cloud in OOB The link you have shared only talks about how to add/delete/remove CA Certificate. Also please guide us on the code/configuration changes to be done for validation of certificate

entry.title: Relatórios do Excel e Análise Interativa
entry.content:
Bom dia,

Estou tentando gerar alguns relatórios pelo Analise Interativa que fica no seguinte caminho: Módulos Relatorio do Excel e Analise Interativa, porém ocorre o seguinte erro: Não é possível abrir janela do Excel; instalar Microsoft Excel e repetir operação. 

Já instalamos o office 2007 e microsoft365 no servidor template, mesmo assim ocorre a mesma mensagem, gostaria da ajuda de vocês quanto a isso, como resolvo este problema?

 

Atte,
Eduardo Victorino

entry.title: Re: How to Read Data Stored in SAP Datasphere from SAP BW Bridge
entry.content: I tried executing the ABAP Class, but still I am facing the error as per attachment. Can you please assist.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while executing FAGLGVTR
entry.content:
Hi,

Kindly carry forward your balance through T.code FAGLGVTR from start fiscal year to end ( One by one) and after that kindly check it now.

entry.title: Re: Timestamp in SRT_MONI Reflecting When Message Was Fir...
entry.content: init

entry.title: Re: New company code as a copy of an old one
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

Another user asked the same question that I answered recently. 

Please find my answer following the link below:

How to copy customizing settings from a Company code to a new one?

I hope this will help you solve your issue.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Create Purchase Order - Advanced Down Payment Check
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

You have probably already checked this information?

Extensibility for Purchase Order Apps

I have forwarded your question to the experts.

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Wave management
entry.content:
Hi, we have SAP S4 Hana including EWM.

Trucks comes for loading with two different destinations for unloading (2 different customer orders). We release the wave, but the issue is that both customer orders are released in the wave. Is there a possibility to first release wave for one customer order and then remaining wave for the second customer order instead of all at once?

Thank you very much for your response.
